items.q: What weather does an anticyclone (high pressure system) bring?
items.correct: Descending air warms and dries, suppressing cloud formation — so anticyclones bring clear, settled weather: hot in summer, cold and frosty in winter.
items.misconception: High pressure brings stormy weather because the air pushes down hard.
items.random_wrong: Anticyclones bring heavy rainfall because they trap moisture.
items.why: Students conflate 'high pressure' with 'strong weather'. In fact, sinking air evaporates clouds — depressions (low pressure with rising air) bring storms, not anticyclones.

items.q: What is the difference between a four-figure and six-figure grid reference?
items.correct: Four-figure references identify a 1km square (eastings then northings); six-figure references pinpoint a location within that square to 100m accuracy by adding tenths.
items.misconception: Six-figure references are just longer versions of the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Four-figure references are for OS maps and six-figure references are for atlases.
items.why: Accuracy differs by an order of magnitude. Students forget the 'along the corridor and up the stairs' order (eastings before northings) — a common exam error costing easy marks.

items.correct: Cross-border investment where a firm acquires lasting control or ownership of assets in another country (e.g. building a factory, buying a company) — distinct from short-term portfolio investment in shares.
items.misconception: FDI is the same as foreign aid given by governments.
items.random_wrong: FDI is when individuals send money home to their families abroad.
items.why: FDI is private and profit-seeking, not aid (government grants) or remittances (worker income). The control/ownership element distinguishes it from speculative portfolio investment, which can flee rapidly in crises.

items.q: Roughly what share of Mumbai's people live in slums?
items.why: It's 40%. “10%” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is a key Mumbai industry?
items.why: It's Bollywood film. “Coal mining” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximate daily passengers (millions) on Mumbai suburban railway?
items.why: The real figure is 7. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
